DISCOVERY 302, THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF LINDA BARRY (WAGE GARNISHMENT) IS CONTINUED TO 6/1/12 AT 9 AM., ASSUMING THAT JUDGE BEGERT DOES NOT ISSUE A STAY OF ENFORCEMENT OF THE JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE IN THE MEANTIME. THE COURT FINDS THAT MS. BARRY'S HOUSING EXPENSES ARE NOT REASONABLE, BUT GIVES HER 6 MONTHS TO REDUCE THEM. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS DIRECTED TO RELEASE ANY EARNINGS HELD TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR, AND TO STOP GARNISHMENT UNTIL FURTHER ORDER BY THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Claim Of Exemption; Opposition
Off Calendar Dec-01-2011 Continued To Jun-01-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Geoffrey Rotwein, Esq., Law Office, 415-397-0860, for Judgment Creditor
Nancy Conway, Esq., Law Office, 415-241-1140, for Judgment Debtor

Argued and The Claim Of Exemption of Linda Barry (Wage Garnishment) is continued to 6/1/12 at 9 am., assuming that Judge Begert does not issue a stay of enforcement of the judgment in this case in the meantime. The Court finds that Ms. Barry's housing expenses are not reasonable, but gives her 6 months to reduce them. The levying officer is directed to release any earnings held to the judgment debtor, and to stop garnishment until further order by the Court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.

Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Dec-01-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Geoffrey Rotwein, Esq., Law Office, 415-397-0860, for Judgment Creditor
Nancy Conway, Esq., Law Office, 415-241-1140, for Judgment Debtor

Argued and The Claim Of Exemption of Linda Barry (Bank Levy) is granted in part. $934.91 to the judgment creditor. $4460.49 to be returned and paid to judgment debtor. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.

Ntc Of Claim Of Exemption; Opposition
Off Calendar Oct-21-2011 Continued To Dec-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
PLAINTIFF NELSON BARRY Ntc Of Claim Of Exemption; This matter is continued on the court's own motion to December 1 in the expectation that by that date Judge Begert will have ruled on the motion to enforce settlement in case number 499544. The parties should do everything within their ability to ensure that there is a ruling on the motion to enforce settlement by November 23. A ruling on that motion is likely to impact the ruling on this matter. Order continuing the hearing signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.

A ruling on that motion is likely to impact the ruling on this matter. Order continuing the hearing signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.
